eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects high uncertainty in short-range forecasts for Guangzhou's May 16 high temperature, with model ensembles from the China Meteorological Administration (CMA) and global systems like ECMWF and GFS clustering around 26–28°C amid divergent cloud cover projections. Patchy rain and thundery showers in recent CMA advisories, stemming from strengthening monsoon flow and sea breeze convergence, favor suppressed peaks near 26–27°C by limiting solar insolation and enhancing boundary layer cooling, while 28°C or higher requires clearer skies for urban heat buildup. Historical mid-May highs average 29°C, but current humid conditions (80%+ relative humidity) and recent southern China convective activity tilt odds away from extremes, with new 00Z/12Z model runs expected to resolve discrepancies before resolution.

markets.question: OGC Nice vs. FC Metz: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Ligue 1 game between OGC Nice and FC Metz, scheduled for May 17 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if OGC Nice and FC Metz combine to score 3 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 3, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligue1.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: OGC Nice vs. FC Metz - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Ligue 1 game, scheduled for May 17 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: OGC Nice, sitting 15th in Ligue 1 with 31 points after a 1-1 home draw against Lens in matchweek 32, face a critical home clash against relegated bottom-side FC Metz on May 17 at Allianz Riviera. Desperate to secure survival and avoid the relegation playoff—especially ahead of their pivotal away trip to 16th-placed Auxerre—the hosts hold a strong historical edge, unbeaten in recent home fixtures versus Metz and boasting just two losses in 19 such encounters. However, injuries plague Nice, with forward Elye Wahi (thigh), defender Moïse Bombito (lower leg), and others sidelined, mirroring Metz's extensive absences including Habib Diallo (hamstring) and multiple defenders. Metz's poor away form and demotivation post-relegation could favor a low-scoring affair, though Nice's leaky defense (58 goals conceded) adds upset risk.

markets.question: Will there be a run scored in the first inning?: Milwaukee Brewers vs. St. Louis Cardinals
markets.description:
In the upcoming MLB game between the Milwaukee Brewers and St. Louis Cardinals, scheduled for May 5 at 7:45 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if at least one run is scored in the 1st inning by either the Milwaukee Brewers or St. Louis Cardinals.

This market will resolve to "No" if no runs are scored in the 1st inning by either team.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

To know when a postponed game will be played, please check the home team's schedule on MLB.com for the listed team and look for the game described as a makeup game.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official final statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Milwaukee Brewers vs. St. Louis Cardinals
events.description:
In the upcoming MLB game between the Milwaukee Brewers and St. Louis Cardinals, scheduled for May 5 at 7:45PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Milwaukee Brewers" if the Milwaukee Brewers win the game.

This market will resolve to "St. Louis Cardinals" if the St. Louis Cardinals win the game.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, or ends in a tie, this market will resolve 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official final statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the tight NL Central race, St. Louis Cardinals hold a slim edge at 24-17 over the Milwaukee Brewers at 23-17, with recent form showing both clubs splitting their May 4-6 series at Busch Stadium—Cardinals won the opener 6-3 before Brewers' 6-2 finale victory powered by Andrew Vaughn's three-run homer and Jackson Chourio's six-for-nine surge upon return. Brewers remain without Christian Yelich (groin strain, targeting late May) and Brandon Woodruff (shoulder inflammation, bullpen session May 14), testing rotation depth, while Cardinals await Lars Nootbaar's rehab from heel surgery. Upcoming May 25-27 series at American Family Field favors Milwaukee's home splits and bullpen strength amid playoff positioning stakes.
